sn12.48:1.1:
1
At Sāvatthī. Then a brahmin who was a cosmologist
approached the Blessed One … and said to him:
sn12.48:1.2:
2
"How is it, Master Gotama: does all exist?" "‘All exists’: this, brahmin, is the oldest cosmology."
sn12.48:1.3:
3
"Then, Master Gotama, does all not exist?" "‘All does not exist’: this, brahmin, is the second cosmology."
sn12.48:1.4:
4
"How is it, Master Gotama: is all a unity?"
"‘All is a unity’: this, brahmin, is the third cosmology."
sn12.48:1.5:
5
"Then, Master Gotama, is all a plurality?"
"‘All is a plurality’: this, brahmin, is the fourth cosmology.
sn12.48:2.1:
6
Without veering towards either of these extremes, the Tathāgata teaches the Dhamma by the middle … ."
sn12.48:3.1:
7
When this was said, that brahmin said to the Blessed One: "Magnificent, Master Gotama! … From today let Master Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life."
